command: Design a snap-lid box I can push shut and pull open by hand. Plastic, for 3D printing.
scoring_note: base snap box; certifies the T-S1 track.

command: Design a snap-lid storage box about 120 × 90 × 50 mm that pushes shut and pulls open by hand. Plastic.
physics_implied: resolve_snap feasible at the larger size, 3 hooks
scoring_note: dimensional: bigger box + 3 hooks; ⑤ re-resolves within bounds.

command: Design a push-on snap-lid box that closes with no more than 60 N by hand and needs at least 15 N of pull to open.
scoring_note: spec: explicit force window the ⑤ formula chain must hit.

command: Design a snap-lid box that closes with at most 5 N by hand but needs at least 60 N of pull to open.
scoring_note: physically-contradictory: retention/mating ratio the snap geometry cannot achieve.

command: A small hinged box whose lid latches shut at the front. Plastic, for 3D printing.
A hinged box with a 100 × 70 mm lid that latches shut at the front. Plastic.
physics_implied: same mechanism as B1, box in-bounds

command: A hinged box whose lid opens and stays put near 110° — no latch, just a stop.
physics_implied: hinge+stop of B1 (latch removed); stop caps ~109°
scoring_note: constraint: FORBID the latch — the golden visibly drops the snap element.

command: A hinged box whose lid opens 90° and returns closed — no stop tab, no latch.
scoring_note: constraint→EXPECTED_FAIL: the over-centre lid folds flat with no stop (kept as a regression).

command: A hinged box whose lid opens at least 100° and settles closed within 5°.
scoring_note: spec: open-angle floor the P-HINGE gate must clear (real measured 112.4°).

command: Design a crank-operated platform that raises and lowers a load to different heights.
physics_implied: m13 lift V-A 5/5 + P-FULL 5/5 + P-SLIDE V-B 5/5
scoring_note: base crank lift; the Hard benchmark.

command: Design a crank platform that raises a 1 kg load by about 90 mm.
physics_implied: same mechanism as C1; load 1 kg, stroke 90 in-bounds

command: Design a crank lift whose platform holds within 5 mm of its set height when the crank is released, under a 0.5 kg load.
scoring_note: spec: hold-drift ceiling the pawl (P-HOLD) must clear (real measured 3.37 mm).

command: Design a desktop cabinet whose drawer slides out horizontally when you turn a knob.
scoring_note: constraint: HORIZONTAL travel (no gravity-hold) — the drawer alternate, gear NOT over-engineered here.

command: Design a crank lift that holds a 0.5 kg load, but without any gear or ratchet.
scoring_note: constraint-contradiction: a crank→lift needs rot_to_trans, whose ONLY card is rack_pinion; forbidding gear+ratchet leaves no realizer AND no hold.

command: Design a crank lift that raises the platform 500 mm inside a compact desktop frame.
scoring_note: dimensional-exceeds: the requested stroke is beyond the slide/gear param bounds.

command: Design a threaded screw-jack that lifts a load by turning a leadscrew.
scoring_note: out-of-vocabulary: names a host + element we do not have (no leadscrew card/template).
